title: Haumaniastrum rupestre
taxonomy: Haumaniastrum rupestre is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Haumaniastrum, which consists of several species of plants. The plant's classification places it within the order Lamiales and the clade Asterids. Haumaniastrum rupestre is native to the tropical regions of Africa. The species is known to be a component of the diverse plant life in these regions.

intro: Haumaniastrum rupestre, also known as Himalayan tea, is a type of flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
